items.title: Cyclone Narelle tracker map: When will it hit the Queensland coast?
items.summary: See the latest forecast and wind speeds as the system approaches Far North Queensland.

items.title: 'Butchers, bakers and mechanics' among the Ukrainian civilians trained by Australians for the Russia war
items.summary: Rather than depicting the front line, the Australian War Memorial's new exhibition focuses on preparation for war, the people caught in between and the Australians assisting them.

items.title: Kyle Sandilands says he hopes ARN Media contract will be honoured
items.summary: Radio broadcaster Kyle Sandilands says he is considering "many options" after being taken off-air for insulting his co-host and friend of almost 30 years Jackie "O" Henderson.

items.title: Bunbury masseur Anthony Brain sentenced to more than 15 years jail for raping clients
items.summary: A masseur who described himself as a nudist is jailed for 15-and-a-half years for sexually assaulting more than a dozen female clients.

items.title: What are the best ways to save fuel?
items.summary: From fuel-efficient driving to alternative travel modes, we look at practical ways to combat fuel shortages and price hikes.

items.title: Former student jailed for Eden school firebombing as court hears abuse claims
items.summary: A court hears a 59-year-old man who firebombed a South Coast high school with Molotov cocktails claimed to have been sexually abused by two teachers.

items.title: Major banks announce interest rate increase after Reserve Bank lifts cash rate by 0.25 per cent
items.summary: Australia's four major banks respond to the Reserve Bank of Australia's decision to increase the national cash rate to 4.1 per cent.

items.title: Former Iranian footballer fears for athletes' safety as they return to Iran
items.summary: Atefe Moradi knows Iranian regime's playbook well. She played for her home country's national league before moving to Australia, and fears for the safety of the female footballers returning to Iran.

items.title: Man who killed Perth lawyer Alice McShera at Crown Towers sentenced to 19 years in jail
items.summary: Cameron Pearson, who pleaded guilty to killing Perth barrister Alice McShera by bashing her in the head with a champagne bottle, was sentenced to 19 years in jail.

items.title: Former RAAF pilot accused of murdering his wife loses bid for legal costs in breach of bail stoush
items.summary: Robert John Crawford appealed a decision to refuse his legal costs in a District Court battle against Queensland's police commissioner.

items.title: Navigation sounders turned off before Nuyina hit ocean floor, ATSB report notes
items.summary: A preliminary report has revealed new details about how an Australian research vessel hit the ocean floor after navigational echo sounders were turned off to stop interference with scientific sampling.

items.title: What is vaginismus and how do you know if you have it?
items.summary: Madeleine Edwards had never heard of the term vaginismus before she was diagnosed with the condition.

items.title: Federation Council to remove Indigenous flags despite overwhelming community support
items.summary: Despite strong opposition, the council on the NSW-Victoria border will remove Indigenous flags from its chamber after it was revealed there were no protocols to display them.

items.title: Economists call for budget bravery as rising inflation contributes to another RBA rate rise
items.summary: Economist are urging the government make brave budget calls as Australian households prepare to sweat through another rate rise.

items.title: Manildra ordered to pay $170,000 after silos spill grain into Shoalhaven River
items.summary: The Environment Protection Authority has ordered Manildra to spend $170,000 on community projects after its grain silos collapsed into the Shoalhaven River almost two years ago.

items.title: Mortgage repayment calculator: How much extra you could be paying now the cash rate is 4.1 per cent
items.summary: Tell us your loan size, term and interest rate, and our mortgage repayment calculator will work out how much extra you may have to pay on your loan.

items.title: Ipswich woman charged with murder of Katherine Sanowski
items.summary: A 34-year-old woman has been charged over the alleged murder of Springfield woman Katherine Sanowski, who was found in an Ipswich home last month.

items.title: Identity of street artist Banksy revealed in new investigation
items.summary: An in-depth investigation drawing on court files, photos and public records reports the world-famous anonymous artist is a Bristol-born man who later adopted a new identity.

items.title: Cadia goldmine pollution allegations lead to class action against Newmont
items.summary: A small farming community in central west NSW is taking on mining giant Newmont, alleging a goldmine at Cadia, near Orange, has contaminated their agricultural assets.

items.title: MAGA's civil war over Donald Trump's conflict in Iran descends into personal attacks
items.summary: A civil war within the MAGA movement is getting more lewd by the day as prominent right-wing figures feud online over the war in Iran.

items.title: Izak Rankine to return for Crows after AFL homophobic slur ban, Swans back Charlie Curnow
items.summary: Izak Rankine is set to play his first AFL match in 2026 since serving a four-match ban for a homophobic slur.

items.title: Fuel crisis forces WA resources company Blue Cap Mining to send workers home
items.summary: Fuel shortages linked to the Middle East war are forcing a WA gold miner to stand down workers, exposing how smaller operators are being squeezed while major miners maintain supply.

items.title: Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of deadly air strike on drug rehab centre in Kabul
items.summary: There are fears of heavy casualties as Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of hitting a treatment centre for drug addicts in the capital, Kabul, and killing civilians.

items.title: Scientists say summer of weather whiplash points to driving force of climate change
items.summary: Scientists warn sharp swings from extreme heat to flooding this summer signal climate change is overtaking other weather drivers.

items.title: Diesel thefts rise in regional NSW as prices soar amid Middle East war
items.summary: The NSW Police Rural Crime Team is reporting a spike in diesel being stolen from landholders amid soaring prices.

items.title: Bondi Beach gunman Naveed Akram's family fear for their safety, lawyer says
items.summary: A lawyer has revealed Naveed Akram's mother and siblings have been targeted with abusive messages, death threats, stalking and pork chops being thrown into their driveway.

items.title: Cliff collapse at Port Willunga prompts Adelaide council warning
items.summary: A South Australian mayor says no-one was hurt when boulders fell from a cliff face at a popular beach in Adelaide's south.

items.title: Eurogliders founder Bernie Lynch dies age 72
items.summary: Australian music icon Bernie Lynch wrote all of Eurogliders's biggest songs, including the smash hit Heaven (Must Be There).

items.title: Bluesfest collapse leaves merchandise supplier $65k out of pocket
items.summary: The merchandise suppliers were due to deliver more than 6,000 T-shirts and other branded items this week to be sold at the famous Byron Bay festival over Easter.

items.title: Travel plans disrupted by the Middle East war? Here's what your insurance covers
items.summary: War-linked travel disruptions remain largely excluded under standard travel insurance policies, experts say.

items.title: Petrol price rise amid war in the Middle East adds to cost-of-living focus in SA state election
items.summary: Increased costs for petrol, food and housing are front and centre when it comes to how families are choosing to vote in the upcoming South Australian state election.

items.title: China unveils next round of green energy ambitions in five-year plan
items.summary: China is adopting green energy at a scale hard to imagine for many other countries, with the country's latest five-year plan further signalling its intention to reduce its dependency on foreign oil.

items.title: In UAE, life goes on amid Iran's attacks but the future is uncertain
items.summary: Amid Iran's missile and drone attacks, a weird sense of normality in Dubai and Abu Dhabi sits awkwardly with the news coverage of burning buildings and reports of strikes on airports.

items.title: WA farmer plans 2027 run across Canada to raise rural mental health awareness
items.summary: A Wheatbelt farmer will attempt to run the entire length of Canada next year, aiming to cover 6,500 kilometres in less than 150 days.

items.title: Why I'm taking a break from my marriage — in the backyard
items.summary: After almost five decades of marriage, I needed a break. And since enjoying my new-found freedom, I'm not sure I'll ever go back.

items.title: Queen Mary and King Frederik of Denmark arrive in Melbourne
items.summary: The king and queen of Denmark will arrive in Melbourne for the first of two days of royal engagements as part of their Australian state visit.

items.title: Varroa mite investigation finds illegal imports unlikely but fails to find source of deadly incursion
items.summary: Australia's leading biosecurity experts have been unable to trace the source of the of the bee-killing varroa mite incursion which has changed the face of beekeeping and pollination in Australia.

items.title: Russian 'shadow fleet' gas tanker adrift in Mediterranean poses 'imminent' threat
items.summary: Italy, France and seven other nations say a Russian liquefied natural gas tanker adrift in the Mediterranean represents an ecological threat, urging swift action.

items.title: Two men accused of raping woman who believed they were Uber drivers
items.summary: Two men are on trial in the Northern Territory Supreme Court, accused of sexually assaulting a young woman in her apartment for eight hours after picking her up on a night out while she was waiting for an Uber.

items.title: Only private midwifery practice in WA's Kimberley shuts, leaving mothers with limited options
items.summary: The only private midwifery practice in Broome is being forced to close because it is no longer sustainable to keep operations going.

items.title: Cheaper, cleaner copper technology offers a lifeline as Mount Isa weighs uncertain future
items.summary: Copper production in outback Queensland is facing a ticking clock, but researchers say a new refining process developed at the University of Queensland could slash production costs.

items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: Timothée Chalamet charms cinema-goers but can't crack the Academy
items.summary: At the beginning of 2025, Timothée Chalamet was the hot pick for the Best Actor Oscar for his turn in Marty Supreme. But a lot can change in a few months.

items.title: Chelsea receives record Premier League fine for historical breaches of financial rules
items.summary: Chelsea are fined a record total of $20.2 million for historical breaches of the Premier League's financial regulations, but avoid a points deduction.

items.title: RBA hikes cash rate to 4.1 per cent in a split decision — as it happened
items.summary: The Reserve Bank has raised the cash rate to 4.1 per cent, its second hike in as many months as inflationary pressures continue.

items.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle expected to bring severe impacts to Far North Queensland
items.summary: The tropical low in the Coral Sea is now officially a cyclone, with the system expected to bring destructive winds and heavy rain when it reaches the coast.

items.title: Australian coach and former player Marinko Matosevic cops doping ban after admitting to blood transfusion
items.summary: Former world number 39 Marinko Matosevic receives a four-year doping ban from tennis's global integrity agency, which he had labelled "corrupt" during its investigation.

items.title: Energy minister says panic buying is 'very much' behind fuel shortages — as it happened
items.summary: Energy Minister Chris Bowen says panic buying is "very much" the cause of fuel shortages in Australia, particularly in regional communities.

items.title: Graziers fork out thousands of dollars at Jerilderie Working Dog Auction
items.summary: The average price for a working dog at a recent auction in southern NSW was $9,500, but trainers insist they are a good investment.

items.title: Victorian duck hunters warned about littering ahead of 2026 season
items.summary: Duck hunters are being warned not to leave rubbish behind ahead of the 2026 hunting season, with a duck rescue group saying more needs to be done about it.

items.title: Deadline arrives for Kyle Sandilands to respond to misconduct claims after Jackie O tirade
items.summary: Almost one month on from the explosive argument between its star hosts, the Kyle and Jackie O Show is expected to learn its fate on Tuesday.

items.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: What time do the Matildas play? How to watch and follow Australia vs China
items.summary: The Matildas are in the final four at the Women's Asian Cup, and take on the defending champion China in the first semifinal tonight. Here's how to follow the game at Perth Stadium.

items.title: Israel says two top Iranian officials killed in air strikes in blow to Tehran leadership — as it happened
items.summary: Israel says it has killed Iran's security chief Ali Larijani, one of the country's most senior officials. Read how events unfolded.

items.title: NSW government to introduce new laws to combat LGBTQIA+ hate crimes
items.summary: Offenders convicted of hate crimes against the LGBTQIA+ community in New South Wales could face up to seven years behind bars under new legislation being introduced to parliament.

items.title: Employers reveal excluding candidates due to mental illness, disability and age
items.summary: Seven in 10 employers reveal they are excluding candidates in certain groups, including those who live with a long-term illness, disability, mental illness or are over 55, according to a new survey.

items.title: Armed drones are cheap, easy to make and deadly — and they're changing the shape of conflicts
items.summary: Defence analysts say the early lessons of the Iran conflict are fairly clear: drone warfare is here to stay and Australia needs to rapidly improve its counter-drone capabilities.

items.title: The RBA rate hike that will do nothing to curb inflation
items.summary: As Reserve Bank board members gather for the second day of meetings this morning, they already would have been peppered with dire warnings about the potential impact of the energy shock.

items.title: Tasmanian duck hunters blame protesters for burning shooting shelters at Moulting Lagoon
items.summary: Tasmanian duck hunters believe their shooting shelters, known as hides, are being burnt down by protesters, with 80 destroyed in the past four years.

items.title: A DNA test, a match and a decades-old IVF mix-up
items.summary: When Sasha Szafranski matched with a stranger on a genealogy website, she uncovered an IVF mix-up that had been hidden for 30 years.

items.title: The world still interprets war through an American lens
items.summary: For much of the past century, the world has learned to interpret war through categories shaped by American power.

items.title: Iranian women's football team begin journey home after five withdraw asylum claims in Australia
items.summary: The Iranian women's football team leave Malaysia for the Middle East after five players withdraw their claims for asylum in Australia.

items.title: White House chief of staff Susie Wiles diagnosed with breast cancer
items.summary: US President Donald Trump announces her diagnosis on Truth Social, saying her prognosis "is excellent".

items.title: Germany says Iran war 'not a matter for NATO', as Trump demands Strait of Hormuz help
items.summary: Donald Trump tells US allies to "get involved quickly" in unblocking the Strait of Hormuz shipping channel, while warning members of NATO the alliance faces a "very bad" future if they do not do their bit.

items.title: Should data centres fund their own power generation?
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Teenager who allegedly handed knife to killer pleads not guilty to murder
items.summary: A teenage boy has pleaded not guilty to handing a knife to a friend who fatally stabbed a man in Logan in 2023.

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Return of the mums, retired shooter winds back the clock and Kiwis make hot start at Firebirds
items.summary: The world's best goal keeper has sparked an important conversation about postpartum depression in her return to the Super Netball league.
